keep.reason: Most comprehensive - checks Type==KMS AND KeyId exists (superset of other encryption checks)

remove.reason: Subset of xray_store_encrypted - only checks Type==KMS, missing KeyId check

keep.reason: Most comprehensive encryption check - checks Type==KMS AND KeyId exists

remove.reason: CRITICAL MISCONFIGURATION: Rule name suggests RBAC/access control check, but actually checks encryption config (Type==KMS). This is a logic error - rule should be checking access control, not encryption.
CRITICAL MISCONFIGURATION: Rule name suggests retention check, but actually checks encryption config (Type==KMS). This is a logic error - rule should be checking retention settings, not encryption.

potential_misconfigurations.issue: Rule name indicates RBAC/least privilege access control check, but actually checks encryption configuration (get_encryption_config → Type==KMS). This is a fundamental logic mismatch.
potential_misconfigurations.recommendation: Either fix the rule to check access control (IAM policies, resource policies) OR remove this rule if encryption check is redundant with xray_store_encrypted
potential_misconfigurations.expected_check: Should check IAM policies, resource policies, or access control settings for least privilege

potential_misconfigurations.issue: Rule name indicates retention days minimum check, but actually checks encryption configuration (get_encryption_config → Type==KMS). This is a fundamental logic mismatch.
potential_misconfigurations.recommendation: Either fix the rule to check retention settings (group settings, retention period) OR remove this rule if encryption check is redundant with xray_store_encrypted
potential_misconfigurations.expected_check: Should check group settings or retention configuration for minimum retention days

recommendations.description: Fix 2 rules with critical misconfigurations - rule names don't match actual checks. Both incorrectly check encryption instead of their intended purpose (RBAC and retention)
recommendations.impact: Prevents incorrect compliance reporting and ensures rules check what they claim to check

recommendations.description: Merge encryption checks - xray_store_encrypted is more comprehensive (checks Type AND KeyId)
recommendations.impact: Reduces duplicate encryption checks, improves maintainability

aws.xray.trace.xray_store_encrypted.note: Most comprehensive - checks both Type and KeyId

aws.xray.samplingrule.xray_storage_encry.note: Subset of xray_store_encrypted - only checks Type

aws.xray.trace.rbac_least_privilege.note: MISCONFIGURATION: Name suggests RBAC check but actually checks encryption

aws.xray.trace.retention_days_minimum.note: MISCONFIGURATION: Name suggests retention check but actually checks encryption

aws.xray.samplingrule.xray_rules_present.note: Unique rule - checks sampling rules exist

aws.xray.samplingrule.rbac_least_privile.note: Unique rule - checks sampling rule ARNs exist (legitimate RBAC check)

compliance_analysis.note: No explicit compliance standards found in any metadata YAML files

rule_logic_issues.summary: 2 rules have critical logic mismatches where rule names don't match actual checks performed

issues_fixed: Fixed metadata_mapping.json for aws.xray.trace.xray_store_encrypted: Changed logical_operator from null to all (YAML has multiple conditions)
Fixed metadata_mapping.json for aws.xray.samplingrule.xray_rules_present: Changed field_path from SamplingRule to SamplingRuleRecords[].SamplingRule.RuleName to match YAML (checks item.RuleName)
